title: carex duriuscula subsp. rigescens
intro: Carex duriuscula subsp. rigescens, also known as the rough sedge, is a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. It is primarily used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant are believed to include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, Carex duriuscula subsp. rigescens is typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
